authority_rule: The canonical binary private-state artifact is authority; JSON is only a redacted projection for review surfaces.
projection_rule: Projection must be derived from canonical bytes, must carry the source hash, and must never be accepted as private-state authority.
serialization_rules: field numbers are encoded in ascending order
strings are UTF-8 with u32 length prefixes
repeated sections preserve declared order after validation
content hashes are sha256 over the exact canonical byte stream
schema_evolution_rules: field numbers 1 through 15 are reserved for v1 identity, lineage, projection, ledger, and policy fields
unknown required fields fail closed until a migration policy accepts them
future protobuf/prost bindings must preserve the same field meanings and hash input boundaries
schema migration must emit a continuity witness before later WPs may treat the successor as active
compatibility_notes: v0.90.2 JSON citizen, wake, quarantine, and Observatory artifacts are inheritance evidence, not durable private-state authority
v0.90.3 WP-03 records the canonical format boundary before signed envelopes, local sealing, and append-only ledger work add stronger authority layers
JSON projections remain useful for operators only when they hash-link back to canonical bytes and preserve redaction boundaries
non_claims: does not implement signed envelopes or trust-root validation
does not implement encryption, sealed checkpoint storage, or cloud enclaves
does not implement append-only lineage replay or duplicate-head quarantine
does not claim first true Godel-agent birth
